what_input_feature: A small (sub-1.4KB) PNG with a 32-byte cHRM chunk placed before any IDAT/PLTE, where the 4 bytes at cHRM-data offset 12 (buf+12) decode through png_get_fixed_point to PNG_FIXED_ERROR (0x7fffffff), driving the redy==PNG_FIXED_ERROR comparison true.
why_winner_satisfies: naive's winning seeds are short (656-1314 bytes), invoke png_handle_cHRM 64 times vs 20, and actually take the L1280 true side 12 times (T=12) while never sinking budget into the IDAT/read-row decode machinery; pure havoc byte/word/dword arithmetic mutators repeatedly perturb the redy field until one execution lands the 0x7fffffff sentinel.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog's blocking seeds are large (738-8759 bytes, several 4902-8771) carrying full IDAT image streams; they pour execution budget into png_read_finish_row/png_combine_row/png_read_IDAT_data (hundreds of L hits, zero W hits), reach cHRM only 20 times, and never produce redy==PNG_FIXED_ERROR (T=0 at L1280).
mechanism_attribution: naive wins by raw havoc throughput: with no CMP interception tax it runs more executions per second and its byte/Word/Dword arithmetic mutators stochastically hit the 4-byte 0x7fffffff sentinel in the redy slot, whereas cmplog's per-CMP callback + I2S buffer processing cost lowers throughput and its I2S substitution offers no help (PNG_FIXED_ERROR is an internal sentinel produced by overflow logic, not a value present as an input-byte pre-image to splice).

what_input_feature: Same structural requirement: a cHRM-bearing PNG whose redy fixed-point at buf+12 evaluates to PNG_FIXED_ERROR, reaching the L1280 equality comparison so it can resolve true.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 and takes the L1280 true side (hitcount 4 on the branch), aided by the CMP_MAP gradient that gives partial-progress credit on the integer comparison xy.redy == PNG_FIXED_ERROR, letting Hamming-distance-closer inputs survive in the corpus and be refined toward the exact 0x7fffffff value.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (lacking the value_profile CMP_MAP feedback) gets no partial-progress signal on the redy comparison, resolves only 1/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch, and stays blocked an average 22.30h.
mechanism_attribution: the one-technique delta vs cmplog is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: per-CMP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward inputs whose redy value is bitwise closer to PNG_FIXED_ERROR, so the corpus accumulates near-misses and arithmetic mutation walks them to the exact sentinel — cmplog's edge-only feedback treats every not-yet-equal redy as identical.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_consonant_syllable(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: A KANNADA-script buffer whose current syllable spans >=3 glyphs so that start+3<=end holds; produced by long repeated multi-byte glyph runs (e.g. runs of 0x74 'tttttttt' and repeated 'cb 0c 00 00' structures) that decode into syllables of length >=3.
why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves the branch in all 10 trials with avg hitcount 52; its winning seeds carry long homogeneous runs (Seed 3/5 'ssssssssss', Seed 1 'tttttttt') that produce multi-glyph syllables, and naive's pure havoc byte-insert/copy mutators grow input length unconstrained, frequently producing >=3-glyph syllables.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks in 8/10 trials with avg hitcount 0; at the blocker line L479 the loser takes True 0 times (T=0 F=15) while still reaching the Kannada script check at L478 (T=15), i.e. it enters Kannada syllables but they are never long enough; cmplog's loser seeds are short (10-60 bytes) and its I2S substitution pins bytes to intercepted CMP operands rather than lengthening the input.
mechanism_attribution: naive wins by pure havoc length-growth; this is a branch where the I2S technique HURTS: cmplog (loser) spends mutation budget on I2SRandReplace operand substitutions that do not increase syllable length, while naive's unconstrained byte-insert/copy havoc readily reaches start+3<=end. The differentiating winner mechanism is havoc-only mutation, not comparison solving.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_consonant_syllable(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: Same program-side condition: a KANNADA-script syllable spanning >=3 glyphs (start+3<=end). The axis distinguishing the two fuzzers is the value_profile (CMP_MAP gradient) feedback, not the byte feature itself.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in 8/10 trials (avg hitcount 2) vs cmplog's 1/10; its winning seeds (Seed 1/2, vpc trial 1) carry the same long glyph runs ('tttttttt', repeated 'G 0c 00 00') that build >=3-glyph syllables, and vpc adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets that reward progress toward branch-relevant comparison states cmplog cannot see.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser in this pair too) blocks in 8/10 trials and never takes the True side at L479 (T=0 F=15); lacking the CMP_MAP gradient feedback it gets no partial-progress reward for inputs that approach the >=3-glyph syllable length and stays stuck on short inputs.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog wins over cmplog via the CMP_MAP gradient: the Hamming/value-profile comparison feedback gives a corpus-add signal for inputs that make incremental progress toward the comparison/length states gating the branch, feedback that bare cmplog lacks. The differentiating winner technique is the CMP_MAP gradient.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_consonant_syllable(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: A byte sequence decoding to an old-spec Indic syllable (indic_plan->is_old_spec true) where, after the base consonant, position i carries indic_category() == I_Cat(H) (a Halant), so the true side of line 678 is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves 10/10 with avg hitcount 34 on the branch; its winning seeds reach the true side via pure havoc/token byte mutation that happens to assemble the H-after-base glyph layout (e.g. seed bd95f0d9... carries an 'antd'/'do-hant-mo' style structural payload built by Crossover/Delete/Neg/Insert/BitFlip ops).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 1/10 and the branch hitcount is 0; its I2SRandReplace mutator overwrites byte runs to match intercepted CMP operands, displacing the carefully-positioned Halant category bytes so the post-base scan at line 677-678 never sees an H, leaving the true side unhit (DIVERGENT BRANCHES line 678 L: T=0 F=17).
mechanism_attribution: Pair winner is naive (no I2S); the differentiating technique I2S is what the LOSER has. Plain havoc/token byte mutation in naive assembles the structural glyph sequence, while cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutions actively perturb the same bytes toward CMP operands, so the winner is enabled by havoc-only mutation, not by any comparison-solving.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_consonant_syllable(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: Same structural requirement (old-spec Indic syllable with a Halant glyph after the base consonant taking the true side of line 678), reachable when the value-profile gradient feedback rewards inputs whose category/codepoint bytes approach the H-after-base layout.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 (avg hitcount 2); its CMP_MAP gradient buckets give partial credit for byte sequences that move toward matching the comparison at line 678, retaining intermediate inputs that the loser discards.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (no value_profile) resolves 1/10 and blocks 9/10 with branch hitcount 0; lacking the CMP_MAP gradient it gets only all-or-nothing edge feedback and its I2S substitutions disrupt the H bytes, so it never retains the near-miss inputs needed to reach the true side.
mechanism_attribution: vpc beats cmplog and the differentiating technique is value_profile, so the enabling mechanism is the CMP_MAP gradient: Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward partial progress toward the H-category comparison at line 678, letting vpc climb to the true side where plain edge feedback (cmplog) stalls.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_syllable_indic(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: Byte content that, after Indic-shaper categorization, yields a syllable whose low nibble equals indic_symbol_cluster; reached by broad havoc byte mutation that varies the codepoint/category bytes rather than by CMP substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves 10/10 with hitcount 88; its havoc/token stack (DwordAddMutator, ByteInterestingMutator, TokenReplace etc.) randomly perturbs the codepoint bytes and lands category-bearing inputs, e.g. the naive seed prefixed f3 a8 00 00 with long a2 runs.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 9/10 with hitcount 0; its added I2SRandReplace stage finds no input-byte CMP to substitute because syllable_type is an internal nibble classification, so I2S energy is wasted and naive's plain havoc out-explores it.
mechanism_attribution: The syllable_type switch is a category dispatch, not an integer/string CMP against an input constant, so cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitution gains no traction; the win is plain havoc byte mutation (havoc-only) that naive spends fully on category exploration while cmplog dilutes effort on fruitless I2S.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_syllable_indic(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: Same indic_symbol_cluster syllable requirement; value_profile_cmplog additionally gets CMP_MAP-bucket feedback that rewards partial progress in the comparisons feeding the categorization path.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 vs cmplog's 1/10 over the same branch; its instrumentation adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog, giving a Hamming-distance signal cmplog lacks.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has only edge-bucket feedback and the I2S stage; with no gradient signal it stays blocked 9/10 at hitcount 0 while vpc advances.
mechanism_attribution: The added technique vpc has over cmplog is the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance feedback buckets), which rewards inputs closer to category-relevant comparison operands, enabling vpc to reach indic_symbol_cluster where bare cmplog cannot.

what_input_feature: A multi-record buffer whose bytes decode to a Myanmar consonant syllable with at least one glyph carrying myanmar_category VPre, built from the repeated 4-byte little-endian record pattern (e.g. 84 10 00 00 / 1a 10 00 00) seen in winner seeds.
why_winner_satisfies: naive's plain havoc/token stack iterates freely over the periodic record layout (size grows 20->57 bytes, mutation_op chains like BytesExpandMutator/CrossoverInsertMutator), accumulating enough VPre-categorized glyphs that the true side fires 46 times (W T=46) and naive resolves 10/10.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog spends its execution budget on CMP-buffer processing and I2SRandReplace substitutions that do not help reach a category-table classification (no literal CMP operand maps to the VPre category), so it blocks 8/10 with the true side never taken (L T=0 F=20) and only hitcount 1.
mechanism_attribution: naive wins because it lacks I2S overhead/misdirection: with no CmpLogObserver to drive substitutions, its havoc budget is spent purely on byte/length mutation of the periodic record array, which is what actually produces a VPre glyph; the I2S technique present in cmplog provides no traction on a category-table lookup and is net-negative here.

what_input_feature: The same VPre-bearing Myanmar consonant syllable, but reached under value-profile gradient feedback that rewards partial progress toward the categorized record bytes (the 0x10 second-byte / d7 run structure).
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog; the winner seeds (all fuzzer=value_profile_cmplog) reach the branch with hitcount 6 and resolve 9/10, showing the gradient feedback retains intermediate inputs approaching the VPre-producing record layout.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has the same I2S mutator but NO CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so intermediate inputs that are byte-wise closer to a VPre record earn no new-coverage reward and are discarded; it blocks 8/10 with avg duration 17.80h and hitcount 1.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient gives credit for partial matches on the comparison values that gate category classification, letting the corpus climb toward a VPre-categorized glyph; cmplog without that gradient gets no such intermediate reward.

what_input_feature: Bytes that decode to a valid Myanmar consonant syllable whose glyph stream includes a VPre (left-matra) category item, so line 236 assigns POS_PRE_M and the blocker true side at line 280 fires; the byte layout is a stream of little-endian 16-bit-ish category records (winner seeds are dense runs of 0x84 0x10 / 0xd7 patterns).
why_winner_satisfies: naive's pure havoc+token byte mutation drifts the input into a category mix containing VPre: the loser's I2S substitutions actively pin operand bytes to CMP-derived values that never produce a VPre, so naive (no I2S) explores more freely and reaches the VPre arm; line 234 shows the VPre test is taken (W T=46) and line 280 fires (W T=46).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks on 8/10 trials with avg 19.10h and hitcount 1; its I2S substitutions lock operand bytes onto logged CMP values that decode to non-VPre categories, so line 234 VPre test is never true (L T=0) and POS_PRE_M is never assigned, leaving line 280 true-side at L T=0.
mechanism_attribution: Pair 1 winner is naive, which has NO I2S; the delta technique I2S is detrimental here. The enabling mechanism is plain havoc-only byte mutation: cmplog's I2SRandReplace over-constrains operand bytes toward logged comparison values, narrowing exploration away from the VPre category, while naive's unconstrained havoc drifts into a VPre-bearing syllable.

what_input_feature: Same program-side condition: a Myanmar consonant syllable carrying a VPre left-matra glyph that triggers the POS_PRE_M assignment at line 236, satisfying the line-280 true side.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance) feedback on top of cmplog's I2S, so partial progress toward the category-byte values that decode to VPre is rewarded as new coverage; it resolves 9/10 with the VPre/POS_PRE_M chain reached, and its winning seeds are dense 0x84 0x10 / 0xd7 category runs.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but lacks the CMP_MAP gradient; with no reward for partial byte-distance progress it stalls on the same category-byte gate (blocked 8/10, hitcount 1) and never assembles a VPre-bearing record, so line 280 true side stays at L T=0.
mechanism_attribution: Pair 2 delta is value_profile; both fuzzers have I2S, so the discriminating technique is the CMP_MAP gradient feedback that vpc has and cmplog does not. The gradient rewards incremental Hamming progress toward the multi-byte category values that decode to a VPre glyph, letting vpc cross the gate cmplog stalls on.

function: hb-ot-shaper-use.cc:find_syllables_use(hb_buffer_t*)::{lambda(hb_pair_t<unsigned int, hb_glyph_info_t const&>)#1}::operator()(hb_pair_t<unsigned int, hb_glyph_info_t const&>) const
what_input_feature: An input that drives the USE shaper to build a glyph buffer containing a ZWNJ glyph (USE(ZWNJ)) followed at some later index by a glyph that is not_ccs_default_ignorable and not a Unicode mark, so the false-branch return path is reached; in practice readable script/language tag bytes (e.g. 'srch', 'jy-hant', 'an-hant', 'nt-hk').
why_winner_satisfies: naive winning seeds carry intact, human-readable script/language tag substrings ('srch' at offset 0x1d-0x20, 'jy-hant' / 'an-hant', 'nt-hk') that map to real USE categories and populate the buffer with a ZWNJ-plus-following-glyph structure, letting the lambda iterate the loop and exit on the false side.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocking seeds are dominated by repeated CMP-derived byte runs ('00 72 10' x4, '0c 18 00 00 0c 20') that do not assemble into the categorized glyph sequence; without a real ZWNJ-then-non-ignorable structure the loop body never reaches the false-branch return.
mechanism_attribution: naive's plain havoc/token byte mutation is free to keep and recombine the intact tag substrings that survive in the corpus; cmplog's I2SRandReplace continually overwrites byte offsets with operands harvested from the CMP buffer, displacing the readable tag bytes and steering the corpus toward CMP-satisfying noise rather than the structural glyph sequence — so the WINNER (naive) succeeds by NOT applying I2S substitution. This is havoc-only.

function: hb-ot-shaper-use.cc:find_syllables_use(hb_buffer_t*)::{lambda(hb_pair_t<unsigned int, hb_glyph_info_t const&>)#1}::operator()(hb_pair_t<unsigned int, hb_glyph_info_t const&>) const
what_input_feature: Same structural requirement (ZWNJ glyph followed by a non-ignorable non-mark glyph in the buffer), reached by gradually accreting the multi-byte tag/category bytes that select the right USE categories.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets that reward partial progress toward the byte patterns selecting ZWNJ + following non-ignorable glyph categories, so it accretes the needed structure and resolves the false side in 8/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: Plain cmplog has only edge-bucket feedback plus I2S substitution and no Hamming/prefix gradient; it gets no reward for partial matches, the I2S substitutions overwrite progress, and it stays blocked in 9/10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient feedback gives a smooth Hamming-distance signal that incrementally guides the input toward the byte combination producing the ZWNJ-plus-non-ignorable glyph structure, which plain cmplog lacks. This is the CMP_MAP gradient mechanism.

what_input_feature: A byte sequence that, after the USE shaper maps each glyph to a use_category(), produces the category sequence that drives the Ragel state machine into the numeral-cluster accepting transition (trans_action 14 -> use_numeral_cluster at line 993). No exact magic constant is compared; the gate is structural over a run of category-classified bytes.
why_winner_satisfies: naive's pure havoc/token stack (e.g. BytesInsertCopyMutator, WordAddMutator, ByteRandMutator) churns the multi-byte glyph records freely, and in 8/10 trials lands a category run that hits case 14 (winner hitcount=5 on the branch); the winning seeds show repeated structured runs like '0c 20 00 00' record patterns that the category classifier accepts.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog spends its mutation budget on I2SRandReplace substitutions keyed to intercepted integer CMPs; the USE category gate is not an exact-value CMP it can solve, so I2S substitutions are off-target, polluting the corpus and starving the havoc churn that actually reaches case 14 (cmplog resolved 0/10, blocked 10/10, hitcount 0).
mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive, which lacks I2S entirely. Reaching the numeral cluster needs broad structural byte mutation of the glyph stream, which havoc supplies. cmplog's added I2SRandReplace stage diverts energy toward non-existent exact-match comparison targets, so adding the I2S technique strictly hurts here (anti-I2S). Mechanism enabling the winner = havoc-only byte mutation.

what_input_feature: The same numeral-cluster category run; the differentiator is that the run is approached incrementally — partial category prefixes that the CMP_MAP gradient can reward as it climbs toward the accepting transition for case 14.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient on top of cmplog's edge+I2S signal, giving partial-progress feedback over the comparison values in the state-machine key lookups (lines 966-967, '_keys[0] <= ... <= _keys[1]'), so near-miss category runs are retained and refined; vpc resolves 8/10.
why_loser_doesnt: plain cmplog has only edge-bucket feedback plus I2S; it gets no gradient credit for category runs that are close-but-not-accepting, so partial progress toward case 14 is discarded and the branch stays blocked 10/10 (hitcount 0).
mechanism_attribution: The winner vpc differs from the loser cmplog by exactly the value_profile technique (CMP_MAP gradient / Hamming-prefix distance feedback). The gradient over the trans-key comparison values lets vpc retain incrementally-closer inputs that converge on the numeral-cluster transition. Mechanism enabling the winner = CMP_MAP gradient.

what_input_feature: A buffer of repeated fixed-stride glyph records (offset 0x00=0x64, 0x01=0xa8, then a 02 1f 01 00 tail) that yields a syllable of length >1 whose first glyph maps to use_category USE(R); the leading-record byte and multiplicity must both hold simultaneously.
why_winner_satisfies: Naive winner seeds 2-5 are built from the repeated record skeleton (e.g. seed 2 '64 a8 00 05 02 1f 01 00 63 a8 00 00 02 1f 01 00'), and the BYTE DIFF shows W concentrates 64(d)x4 at 0x00 and a8(.)x4 at 0x01, so havoc byte-add/inc/delete on this self-similar layout reliably produces >1 USE(R)-led glyphs.
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds carry CMP/token-derived literal runs (e.g. 'true', ';;;;;sn', 'art', repeated 'b8'/'3b' fills) rather than the 64 a8 record stride, so they do not present a USE(R) leading glyph in a length>1 syllable and the true side never fires (loser resolved=0/10, blocked=10).
mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace HURTS here: cmplog spends mutation energy substituting CmpLogObserver operands into the input, dragging it toward literal CMP targets and away from the self-similar repeated-record layout that the structural USE(R) gate needs; naive, lacking I2S, keeps doing plain havoc on the record skeleton and wins. The enabling technique for the winner is havoc-only mutation.

what_input_feature: The same multi-glyph syllable with a USE(R)-category leading glyph (length>1), reached when intermediate equality/category comparisons on the glyph-category path are partially satisfied.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog, so partial progress toward info[start].use_category()==USE(R) is rewarded as new coverage; the winner resolves 9/10 with blocked duration only 5.60h vs the loser's 23.60h, indicating the gradient feedback steers it onto the USE(R) record faster.
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog has the identical I2S mutator and the same edge-bucket feedback but NO CMP_MAP gradient bucket, so it gets no partial-credit signal toward the equality gate and stalls (resolved=0/10, blocked=10, dur 23.60h); the only technique difference between the two arms is value_profile.
mechanism_attribution: CMP_MAP gradient: the value_profile feedback distinguishing vpc from cmplog records Hamming/prefix proximity of the use_category comparison operands, rewarding inputs that get closer to USE(R), which guides corpus growth onto the gate that plain cmplog cannot sense.

what_input_feature: A multi-glyph USE-cluster input where reorder_syllable_use iterates a syllable whose info[i] carries the halant use_category, taking the is_halant_use true branch at line 425. No exact magic-value match against any program constant gates this; it requires populating the buffer with enough varied glyph categories that one resolves to halant.
why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves 10/10 in 0.60h average with W hitcount 45 on the branch. Its pure havoc/token byte mutation broadly perturbs the input so the font-mapped glyph stream reaches a halant-bearing reorder syllable; the W cov overlay shows lines 425/426/429 hit (true side taken).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 10/10 for 23.60h with hitcount 0. The added I2SRandReplace stage spends mutation budget substituting logged CMP operands, which does not help build the structural glyph-category state this branch needs, so cmplog never takes the true side (L 425 shows T=0).
mechanism_attribution: naive's havoc + token stack explores the byte space without CMP introspection; this branch is gated by accumulated structural glyph-category state rather than an exact comparison, so naive's broad mutation reaches it while cmplog's I2S budget is misallocated. Enabling technique = havoc-only.

what_input_feature: Same structural requirement (a halant-category glyph inside a reordered USE syllable), but reached here under value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback which rewards partial progress toward the comparison/category state.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10, blocked only 1, average blocked duration 8.50h with prob_div 0.90 vs cmplog. The CMP_MAP gradient buckets give intermediate-distance credit so the corpus retains seeds that approach the needed glyph-category state.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (no value_profile) blocks 10/10 for 23.60h with hitcount 0; lacking the Hamming/prefix-distance gradient feedback, it gets no intermediate credit toward the structural state and stalls.
mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between the winner value_profile_cmplog and loser cmplog is value_profile, i.e. the CMP_MAP gradient feedback that rewards partial comparison progress and keeps near-miss seeds. Enabling technique = CMP_MAP gradient.

what_input_feature: A structured OpenType font whose parsed buffer reaches the HB_SCRIPT_MALAYALAM switch arm AND carries glyph codepoint 0x0D12 at the current position, so the inner switch hits case 0x0D12u (the true side at line 275).
why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves the true side in 8/10 trials with avg hitcount 5; its plain havoc/token byte mutation perturbs the structured font tables (GSUB/GPOS tags) enough to land the MALAYALAM script and the 0x0D12 codepoint without any wasted CMP-substitution work.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks in 8/10 trials with avg hitcount 0; its loser-blocking seeds are short, unstructured byte blobs that never drive the parser into the MALAYALAM arm, and the added I2SRandReplace stage spends mutation budget substituting CMP operands that do not advance toward this script/codepoint gate.
mechanism_attribution: This is an anti-I2S divergence: naive (no I2S) is the winner over cmplog (I2S added). Plain havoc byte mutation finds the structured font faster than cmplog, whose I2SRandReplace substitutions misdirect effort here. The enabling winner mechanism is therefore lucky/structural havoc, not CMP introspection.

what_input_feature: Same parsed-buffer requirement: HB_SCRIPT_MALAYALAM script with current glyph codepoint 0x0D12 (case 0x0D12u true side). The codepoint equality is the comparison that must be satisfied.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 with hitcount 3; adding the CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance feedback) over cmplog gives partial-credit feedback as the buffer codepoint approaches 0x0D12, letting it climb toward the MALAYALAM script and codepoint match its winning seeds (structured fonts with GSUB/GPOS/GSTB tags) satisfy.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 8/10 with hitcount 0; lacking the CMP_MAP gradient it gets no graded signal toward the codepoint comparison, and its I2S substitution alone does not steer the multi-stage script-then-codepoint dispatch, so the true side stays unhit.
mechanism_attribution: The value_profile delta is the CMP_MAP gradient: vpc's Hamming/prefix-distance CMP_MAP buckets give graded feedback toward the codepoint==0x0D12 comparison, which cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) lacks. This is CMP_MAP gradient, not I2S.
